{"step_by_step_thinking": "Asherman syndrome is characterized by intrauterine adhesions that can affect the endometrium. The documents mention that patients with Asherman syndrome may have light or absent menstrual periods (amenorrhea). Some patients have normal periods based on the surface area of the cavity that is affected. Others have no periods but have severe dysmenorrhea. Hypomenorrhea, which is defined as a scanty menstrual flow, is consistent with the description of light or absent menstrual periods in Asherman syndrome.", "answer_choice": "B"}
